mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in the federal funds rate reduce discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, boosting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment drops below 5%, labor market tightness signals strong consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive SPX returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 5.0% in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, leading to positive equity returns over a 60-day quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals produce negative SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation expectations during a tightening cycle compress equity valuations quickly, causing negative 5-day forward returns as real yields spike.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive when inflation accelerates above threshold during rate hikes in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases, translating to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job creation signals robust economic momentum, supporting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as wage growth fuels consumption.
failure_condition: Sustained job growth above threshold coincides with negative SPX returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with declining real estate activity signal credit tightening and demand contraction, pressuring equities downward over 20 days.
failure_condition: Simultaneous rate hikes and housing declines do not predict negative SPX returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Surging industrial output during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, driving short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: Industrial production surges fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances.
